summary: Set 065 Session 2 of 3: integration-surface spike (SDK vs CLI vs first-party). Hands-on, read-only; no shipping-code change, no release. Surveyed surfaces ON THIS HOST: GitHub Copilot CLI 1.0.51 (present, headless, subscription-authed) and claude -p 2.1.63 (present but REFUSES to nest in a Claude Code session) on PATH; codex+gemini CLIs absent; Claude Agent SDK installable (20 transitive deps + wraps Node CLI); first-party adapter buildable (httpx-only). PROVED a representative pair headless against a 4-file fixture (probeable C9-analog + novel-reasoning latent-TypeError) with a structured-output critique prompt that pastes NO file contents (forces tool use): (1) Copilot CLI (default model claude-sonnet-4.6) caught BOTH bugs 3/3, 5-6 tool calls (4 file reads), billing=subscription (premiumRequests:1/run, $0 marginal), ~18-27s -- model swaps to GPT/Gemini/Opus all plan-gated 'not available' on this seat; (2) first-party tool-loop adapter (Anthropic API via httpx, deterministic-servant read_file/grep/list_dir) caught BOTH 3/3 + a bonus finding, 4 read_file calls, metered ~$0.024/run, ~17-22s. GO verdict: path-aware critique CAN be a routed call. Recommendation: PRIMARY = first-party adapter (best preserves multi-provider control + anti-bias servant ownership + httpx-only footprint; only Anthropic exercised here), ALTERNATIVE = Copilot CLI subscription for seat-holders. VERIFICATION: gpt-5.4 cross-provider, R1 ISSUES_FOUND (1 Major: 'delivers multi-provider deterministically' overstated -> only Anthropic run; 2 Minor: 'full determinism' wording + Copilot default-tools claim) all fixed -> R2 VERIFIED 0 remaining. Verify spend $0.069 + spike metered ~$0.072 + routing $0.002.

reason.specifics: Routed recommendation (gemini-pro, cross-provider, $0.0017): S3 is the final synthesis over the full set's history (S1 bake-off + S2 spike + consensus journal), so the incumbent's context continuity is critical for quality and efficiency; cross-provider verification supplies the independent review, so a fresh-perspective orchestrator is not required.
